title: Fever
uae_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.

source_references.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children

sections.heading: 1. Background
sections.evidence_summary: Defines pediatric fever assessment context and the importance of age, measurement site, appearance, persistence, and underlying disease.

sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment
sections.evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

sections.heading: 7. Referral Criteria
sections.evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

evidence_links.direct_relationship: Directly supports pediatric fever terminology, age, temperature, appearance, persistence, and underlying-condition context.
evidence_links.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines pediatric fever assessment context and the importance of age, measurement site, appearance, persistence, and underlying disease.

evidence_links.direct_relationship: Defines the exact Dubai pediatric telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
evidence_links.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

evidence_links.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, feeding, activity, urine, hydration, vital-sign, and associated-symptom documentation.
evidence_links.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

evidence_links.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped high-risk concern prompts and clinician-documented safety-net or follow-up decisions.
evidence_links.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

unresolved_source_gaps: The baseline viral-fever diagnosis label and viral aliases remain unsupported unless clinician-stated.
Medication, antibiotic, hydration, sick-note, and fixed follow-up content is not promoted by this mapping.
The workflow is broader than DHA telehealth setting.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.

items.text: antipyretic use as per clinician plan
sick note issued as per clinician plan

Parent/guardian reports fever in a [age] for [duration]. [Temperature pattern if reported]. [Feeding and oral intake status]. [Activity level and alertness]. [Urine output]. [Associated symptoms: cough, rhinorrhoea, vomiting, diarrhoea, rash if any]. [Relevant negatives reviewed]. [Additional history].
